classData.comment:
/**
 * @summary Pure policy helpers governing when the wake daemon defers a coalesced digest flush
 * because a heavy GraphLog / data-sync delta is still settling.
 *
 * **Failure mode this guards (the "phantom wake-flood"):** a large GraphLog / data-sync delta тАФ e.g. a
 * multi-thousand-row content sync тАФ commits to SQLite in batches. While those batches are landing, the
 * daemon's per-message read-state lookup (`isMessageReadFor`) transiently under-reports `readAt`, so
 * already-read backlog rows slip past the digest's read filter. The digest then over-counts
 * "N new messages" and can spoof a HIGH priority from that inflated set even though nothing
 * genuinely-new arrived тАФ which trains agents to distrust HIGH wakes.
 *
 * **Mechanism (in `daemon.mjs`):** `pollLoop` flags a heavy delta when a single poll returns an
 * unusually large batch (`isHeavyDeltaPoll`); `flushSubscription` then defers (`shouldDeferFlush`) тАФ
 * keeping the coalesced queue intact and re-arming its timer тАФ until the delta has settled, so the
 * digest is computed against committed read-state. Deferral is capped so a genuine wake is delayed,
 * never withheld.
 *
 * Kept pure (no timers, DB, or daemon state) so the policy is unit-testable in isolation, mirroring the
 * daemon's other focused modules (`queries.mjs`, `instanceResolver.mjs`).
 *
 * @module ai/daemons/wake/flushDeferPolicy
 */
classData.summary:
Pure policy helpers governing when the wake daemon defers a coalesced digest flush
because a heavy GraphLog / data-sync delta is still settling.

**Failure mode this guards (the "phantom wake-flood"):** a large GraphLog / data-sync delta тАФ e.g. a
multi-thousand-row content sync тАФ commits to SQLite in batches. While those batches are landing, the
daemon's per-message read-state lookup (`isMessageReadFor`) transiently under-reports `readAt`, so
already-read backlog rows slip past the digest's read filter. The digest then over-counts
"N new messages" and can spoof a HIGH priority from that inflated set even though nothing
genuinely-new arrived тАФ which trains agents to distrust HIGH wakes.

**Mechanism (in `daemon.mjs`):** `pollLoop` flags a heavy delta when a single poll returns an
unusually large batch (`isHeavyDeltaPoll`); `flushSubscription` then defers (`shouldDeferFlush`) тАФ
keeping the coalesced queue intact and re-arming its timer тАФ until the delta has settled, so the
digest is computed against committed read-state. Deferral is capped so a genuine wake is delayed,
never withheld.

Kept pure (no timers, DB, or daemon state) so the policy is unit-testable in isolation, mirroring the
daemon's other focused modules (`queries.mjs`, `instanceResolver.mjs`).
classData.name: ai/daemons/wake/flushDeferPolicy

classData.description: GraphLog entries observed in a single poll at or above which the delta is treated as a heavy / sync delta in flight. Normal agent activity yields a handful of entries per poll; a data-sync yields thousands тАФ so this threshold sits well above ambient traffic and well below sync magnitude.
classData.name: HEAVY_DELTA_THRESHOLD

classData.comment:
/**
 * Quiet period (ms) of no heavy polls after the most recent heavy poll before per-message read-state is
 * trusted again, and also the re-check interval while deferring. `lastHeavyPollAt` refreshes on every
 * heavy poll, so this window only elapses once the sync genuinely stops producing large batches тАФ it
 * therefore rides out a *sustained* multi-minute heavy op (data-syncs can run many minutes) regardless
 * of total duration. Sized to absorb brief inter-batch gaps within one sync; the cost is a one-time
 * post-sync wake delay of roughly this window.
 * @type {Number}
 */
classData.description: Quiet period (ms) of no heavy polls after the most recent heavy poll before per-message read-state is trusted again, and also the re-check interval while deferring. lastHeavyPollAt refreshes on every heavy poll, so this window only elapses once the sync genuinely stops producing large batches тАФ it therefore rides out a sustained multi-minute heavy op (data-syncs can run many minutes) regardless of total duration. Sized to absorb brief inter-batch gaps within one sync; the cost is a one-time post-sync wake delay of roughly this window.
classData.name: HEAVY_DELTA_SETTLE_MS

classData.comment:
/**
 * Absolute backstop on consecutive deferrals for a single flush. `MAX_FLUSH_DEFERS *
 * HEAVY_DELTA_SETTLE_MS` (~60 min) is a **stuck-signal safety net, NOT a normal-operation limit**: a
 * real heavy op тАФ even an "easily 15-minute" data-sync тАФ settles and flushes well before this, because
 * deferral ends as soon as heavy polls stop (the settle window), not at a fixed time budget. The cap
 * only fires if the heavy-delta signal wedges on (e.g. a poll-batch bug), so a genuine wake is delayed,
 * never dropped indefinitely. (Earlier `10` capped total deferral at ~2.5 min, which would force a flush
 * mid-sync on a multi-minute heavy op and re-expose the very leak this guards тАФ hence the larger net.)
 * @type {Number}
 */
classData.description: Absolute backstop on consecutive deferrals for a single flush. MAX_FLUSH_DEFERS * HEAVY_DELTA_SETTLE_MS (~60 min) is a stuck-signal safety net, NOT a normal-operation limit: a real heavy op тАФ even an "easily 15-minute" data-sync тАФ settles and flushes well before this, because deferral ends as soon as heavy polls stop (the settle window), not at a fixed time budget. The cap only fires if the heavy-delta signal wedges on (e.g. a poll-batch bug), so a genuine wake is delayed, never dropped indefinitely. (Earlier 10 capped total deferral at ~2.5 min, which would force a flush mid-sync on a multi-minute heavy op and re-expose the very leak this guards тАФ hence the larger net.)
classData.name: MAX_FLUSH_DEFERS

classData.comment:
/**
 * @summary Whether a single poll's GraphLog batch marks a heavy / sync delta in flight.
 * @param {Number} deltaSize GraphLog entries returned by one poll.
 * @returns {Boolean} `true` when the batch is at or above {@link HEAVY_DELTA_THRESHOLD}.
 */
classData.summary: Whether a single poll's GraphLog batch marks a heavy / sync delta in flight.

returns.description: `true` when the batch is at or above {@link HEAVY_DELTA_THRESHOLD}.

classData.name: isHeavyDeltaPoll

classData.comment:
/**
 * @summary Whether a coalesced wake flush must defer because a heavy delta is still settling.
 *
 * Returns `true` while within {@link HEAVY_DELTA_SETTLE_MS} of the last heavy poll AND the flush has
 * not yet hit the {@link MAX_FLUSH_DEFERS} cap. Once the settle window elapses (read-state is
 * committed) or the cap is reached (never withhold a genuine wake), returns `false`.
 *
 * @param {Object}  opts
 * @param {Number}  opts.now             Current epoch ms.
 * @param {Number}  opts.lastHeavyPollAt Epoch ms of the most recent heavy poll (`0` when none seen).
 * @param {Number} [opts.deferCount=0]   Number of times this flush has already deferred.
 * @returns {Boolean} `true` тЖТ defer the flush; `false` тЖТ read-state trusted, proceed.
 */
classData.summary:
Whether a coalesced wake flush must defer because a heavy delta is still settling.

Returns `true` while within {@link HEAVY_DELTA_SETTLE_MS} of the last heavy poll AND the flush has
not yet hit the {@link MAX_FLUSH_DEFERS} cap. Once the settle window elapses (read-state is
committed) or the cap is reached (never withhold a genuine wake), returns `false`.

params.description: Epoch ms of the most recent heavy poll (0 when none seen).
params.name: opts.lastHeavyPollAt

params.description: Number of times this flush has already deferred.
params.name: opts.deferCount

returns.description: `true` тЖТ defer the flush; `false` тЖТ read-state trusted, proceed.

classData.name: shouldDeferFlush
